author: bridge-bot
content:
<potuz0> re: **@emile17**
> Correct, I would add that in your construction iiuc you don’t even need a real PQ signature like sphincs. Each validator could instead publicly register hash(secret_seed), and when a validator generates a ZK-STARK of correct derivation, it proves knowledge “in ZK” of the preimage of the hash (same idea as ziggy actually)
yes, right, and also right that they could send many keypairs in advance. There are a number of different tradeoffs though, if they send pubkeys, then those don't need to be later included in messages for verification but an attacker can have minutes to break them (or several minutes if you send many of them). If you send hashes that's fine but then 1) openings need to be included with the aggregate and 2) you don't get the benefit that the anchor seed can be the latest justified checkpoint and thus avoid any after-the-fact breaking of the BLS key and sending an equivocating attestation for a different branch. I'll write down in full the protocol as I am thinking it in the current status and the tradeoffs to forkchoice safety + slashing mechanisms (for example we now slash any attestation even if they are invalid, although slashing only valid ones would be fine, only valid includable attestations can cause trouble, in the above proposal the attacker can create a slashable attestation but only invalid ones). 

At any rate, the crytographers at Offchainlabs (Pranay Achnury, Matteo Campanelli, Rosario Gennaro and  Mahimnha Kelkar) tell me that they have a PQ secure way of doing what I want without a ZK Snark and with less than 1Kb per message, so that's promissing.
